principle: A book is not a product. A book is a curriculum path.

curriculumStages.label: Foundation
curriculumStages.summary: Begin with how systems behave, why they drift, and how to see the whole before optimizing the parts.

curriculumStages.label: Practice
curriculumStages.summary: Turn systems thinking into operational habits, questions, models, and decision patterns.

curriculumStages.label: Trust & Control
curriculumStages.summary: Explore governance, trust, constraints, authority, policy, and control inside real systems.

curriculumStages.label: Application
curriculumStages.summary: Apply the AP model to Zero Trust, cybersecurity architecture, and enterprise decision systems.

curriculumStages.label: Synthesis
curriculumStages.summary: Connect the broader philosophy through field notes, essays, and durable mental models.

books.title: The Operator’s Guide to Complex Systems
books.summary: A field-driven approach to understanding behavior, constraints, failure, adaptation, trust, and control inside real systems.
books.readerOutcome: The reader begins seeing systems as interacting forces rather than isolated events.

books.title: Systems Thinking for Operators
books.summary: A practical guide for seeing systems clearly under uncertainty, friction, incomplete information, and operational pressure.
books.readerOutcome: The reader learns how to ask better questions and build better mental models under pressure.

books.title: Trust, Constraints, and Control
books.summary: An exploration of authority, governance, policy, trust relationships, and systemic control across technical and human systems.
books.readerOutcome: The reader sees trust and control as dynamic system properties shaped by evidence, authority, and constraints.

books.title: The Policy Engine Mindset
books.summary: A practical way to think about policy, decisions, enforcement, and adaptive control in systems that must operate under changing conditions.
books.readerOutcome: The reader understands policy as an operational decision system rather than static documentation.

books.title: Zero Trust Actually Explained
books.summary: A clear, architecture-first explanation of Zero Trust as a decision, evidence, and enforcement problem rather than a product category.
books.readerOutcome: The reader understands Zero Trust as an adaptive authorization system driven by evidence and enforcement.

books.title: Asymmetric Precision
books.summary: The broader philosophy behind AP: seeing hidden systems, making better decisions, and improving difficult organizations with precision.
books.readerOutcome: The reader sees AP as a coherent philosophy for understanding and improving complex systems.

books.title: Field Notes from a Systems Architect
books.summary: A collection of operational observations from systems under pressure and the patterns that become visible through lived complexity.
books.readerOutcome: The reader learns to notice patterns, drift, incentives, and signals inside lived systems.
